back: The principle that every adult citizen (18+) has the right to vote regardless of caste, gender, or religion.
The article of the Indian Constitution that enshrines the principle of universal franchise.
A synonym for franchise; the constitutional right to vote in political elections.
A specific geographical area whose voters elect a representative to a legislative body.
An electoral system where the candidate with the most votes wins, even without a majority (50%+).
Election Commission of India; the independent constitutional body that conducts free and fair elections.
The head of the Election Commission of India.
The Election Commission of India was established in 1950.
Guidelines for candidates/parties to ensure fairness; comes into effect when elections are announced.
ECI head (1990s) known for strict reforms, voter IDs, and making elections transparent and fearless.
Electronic Voting Machine; used for recording and counting votes electronically.
Voter Verifiable Paper Audit Trail; produces a paper record for voters to verify their choices.
None Of The Above; an option to reject all candidates in a constituency.
The body (Elected MPs + Elected MLAs) that elects the President of India.
A system where the legislature has two separate houses (e.g., Lok Sabha and Rajya Sabha).
MP represents a constituency in the national Parliament; MLA represents it in the state assembly.
The Rajya Sabha; it is never dissolved, and 1/3 members retire every 2 years.
The official responsible for conducting the election process in a specific constituency.
A preference-based voting system used for indirect elections like the Rajya Sabha.
Approximately 980 million eligible voters participating across 1 million polling stations.
84 seats for Scheduled Castes (SC) and 47 seats for Scheduled Tribes (ST) in the Lok Sabha.
A traditional scepter signifying sovereignty and the fair rule of the monarch.
A group of two or more parties working together to form a majority government.
